title: Help & Support
quickstart_body:
Quick Start — Your First Bot in 3 Steps Step 1 — Create a bot with BotFather 1. Open @BotFather on Telegram 2. Send /newbot 3. Choose a display name (e.g. My Store Bot) 4. Choose a username ending in bot 5. Copy the token BotFather gives you Step 2 — Add the bot to ChatFuel 1. Tap My Bots → Add Bot 2. Paste the token — ChatFuel registers it instantly Step 3 — Start growing Share your bot link t.me/your_bot to collect subscribers.
What you can do right away: Broadcast messages to all subscribers Build interactive button menus Create forms to collect user data Track subscribers and analytics Generate referral & campaign links Schedule posts in advance Protect content with Captcha & Force-Join
Tap the button below to create your first bot now!
commands_body:
Available Commands Bot Management /start — Show the main menu /mybots — List and manage your bots Subscription /premium — View plans & upgrade Help /help — Show this help menu
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Inside Your Bot (Admin Panel) Open your bot and send /admin to access: Broadcasts — Send messages to subscribers Button Menus — Build interactive navigation Forms — Collect data from users Subscribers — View, tag, and ban users Analytics — Stats and growth insights Referrals — Personal subscriber invite links Polls — Create and send polls to your audience Settings — Welcome message, captcha, force-join Paid Community — Monetise with Telegram Stars Tip: Use the inline buttons — no commands to remember!
tutorials_body:
Tutorials Choose a guide to learn step by step:
Getting Started → Creating your first bot → Understanding bot tokens → Sharing your bot with users Growing Your Audience → Building button menus for navigation → Creating forms to capture leads → Setting up referral links → Force-Join and Captcha protection Engaging Subscribers → Sending broadcasts (text, photo, video) → Scheduling posts in advance → Running polls & quizzes → Paid media and Stars payments Advanced Features → Conditional button visibility → Named campaign links & analytics → A/B testing broadcasts → White-label (Business plan)
Select a topic below to dive in:
faq_body:
Frequently Asked Questions
Is ChatFuel free? Yes — the Free plan gives you 1 bot with up to 500 subscribers. Premium plans unlock more bots, higher limits, and advanced features. How many subscribers can I have? Free: 500 per bot. Basic: 1,000. Advanced: 10,000. Business: unlimited. Can I send broadcasts? Yes! All plans include broadcasting. Free users get 1 broadcast/day; paid plans unlock up to unlimited broadcasts with photos, videos, polls, and inline buttons. Can I create button menus? Yes — all plans include button menus. Free: 1 menu, 3 buttons. Basic: 5 menus, 10 buttons each. Higher plans unlock unlimited menus. Can I collect data with forms? Yes — build multi-step forms with text, number, email, phone, choice, and photo questions. Responses are stored and exportable (paid plans). Can I monetise my bot? Yes — use Telegram Stars to accept one-time payments, subscriptions, paid media, and Star invite links. All available from Basic plan and above. Is my data safe? Absolutely. Bot tokens are encrypted with Fernet. All data is stored securely on Neon PostgreSQL. Can I export subscriber data? Yes — subscriber lists and form responses are exportable on all paid plans. How do I upgrade? Tap Premium in the main menu and choose your plan. Payment is instant via Telegram Stars. Still have questions? Contact our support team!
contact_body: Contact Support Our team is ready to help you with any question. Reach us directly: Open Support Chat You can send us: • Bug reports • Feature requests • Account & billing issues • Technical questions Response times: • Free plan — within 48 hours • Basic / Advanced — within 24 hours • Business — priority queue, same day Community & updates: Follow @ChatFuelUpdates for new features, release notes, and tips. Before writing, check the FAQ — your answer might already be there!
tutorial_first_bot_body:
Tutorial: Your First Bot Part 1: Create the Bot (2 minutes) 1. Open Telegram and search for @BotFather 2. Send /newbot to BotFather 3. BotFather asks: "Choose a name" → Type: My First Bot 4. BotFather asks: "Choose a username" → Type: myfirstbot123_bot (Must end with 'bot', must be unique) 5. BotFather gives you a token like: 1234567890:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqr → Copy this entire token! Part 2: Add to ChatFuel (1 minute) 1. Come back to this chat 2. Send /addbot 3. Paste your token 4. Done! Part 3: Test Your Bot (30 seconds) 1. Go to /mybots in this chat 2. Click on your bot 3. Click "Open Bot" button 4. Your bot admin panel opens!
What You Can Do Now: • Send broadcasts to subscribers • Create button menus • Build forms to collect data • View analytics and subscriber lists • Share your bot link Next Steps: • Share your bot link to get subscribers • Create your first button menu • Send a welcome broadcast • Build a form to collect user info Tip: Keep your bot token secret! It's like a password.
Ready to try? Send /addbot now!
tutorial_tokens_body:
Understanding Bot Tokens What is a Bot Token? A bot token is like a password that lets you control your bot. It looks like: 1234567890:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrsTUVwxyz-1234567 Token Structure: • Numbers before colon: Bot ID • After colon: Secret authentication key • Total length: Usually 45+ characters
Security Rules: 1. Never share your token publicly Don't post on social media Don't commit to GitHub Don't send to strangers 2. Store it safely Use a password manager Keep a backup copy Save in secure notes 3. What if it leaks? → Go to @BotFather immediately → Send /mybots → Select your bot → Click "Revoke Token" → Get new token → Update in ChatFuel Finding Your Token: If you lost it: 1. Go to @BotFather 2. Send /mybots 3. Select your bot 4. Click "API Token" 5. Copy the token Important: • One token per bot • Tokens don't expire (unless revoked) • ChatFuel encrypts tokens in database • You can regenerate tokens anytime
Keep your tokens safe!
tutorial_sharing_body:
Tutorial: Sharing Your Bot Getting Your Bot Link: Your bot link is: t.me/your_bot_username Example: If username is @mycoolbot, link is: t.me/mycoolbot
Best Places to Share: 1. Social Media • Twitter/X • Facebook • Instagram bio • LinkedIn 2. Your Website • Add a button • Put in navigation • Footer link 3. Email • Email signature • Newsletter • Welcome emails 4. Messaging Apps • WhatsApp status • Telegram groups (ask permission first!) • Discord server 5. Offline • Business cards • Flyers • QR codes Tips for Success: Do: • Explain what your bot does • Show the value/benefit • Make it easy to click • Use call-to-action ("Try it now!") Don't: • Spam groups • Send unsolicited messages • Make false promises • Be pushy Example Messages: "Check out my new bot! Get daily motivation at t.me/motivationbot Try it now!" "Need quick recipes? My bot has 1000+ ideas! → t.me/recipebot" Track Your Success: • Use /subscribers to see growth • Ask users how they found you • Try different messages
Ready to share? Go to /mybots and click your bot to get the link!
body: Welcome to ChatFuel Help! Choose a topic below to get started, or tap Contact Support to reach our team.
